+ "Help improve Siri, Dictation, and related features by allowing Apple to store and review what you say to Siri, Dictation, and Translate, including audio and text, on this iPhone, and on any connected Apple Watch, HomePod, or Siri-enabled accessory. You can change this later in Settings. This data is not associated with your Apple Account.\n\n[Internal Only] Devices opted in to Improve Siri and Dictation may also send full IntelligenceFlow transcripts to Apple. This includes unredacted contextual and personal information used in processing requests, including on-screen context, contacts, the content of your Messages or emails, Calendar events, information about personal documents or local search results, and any other information Siri may evaluate as potentially relevant to a request. Some of this data may be used to help develop, design, and improve Siri. The data will be used for evaluation purposes only.\n\nFor search requests, including searches made using Siri, like playing an album or searching for world knowledge, will be stored by Apple and used to improve Search, Siri, and related features. You can turn off Help Apple Improve Search in Settings."
